properties.remark: A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC OBSERVED A SHORT-LIVED WATERSPOUT OVER BIG PINE CHANNEL. THE CONDENSATION FUNNEL DID NOT EXTEND VERY FAR BELOW CLOUD BASE. HOWEVER...A SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED AT THE SEA SURFACE. DURATION LESS THAN 1 MINUTE.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS.
A WIND GUST OF 45 KNOTS...OR 52 MPH...FROM THE WEST-SOUTHWEST WAS MEASURED DURING A THUNDERSTORM AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 149 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVEL.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE OFFICE IN KEY WEST.
A WIND GUST OF 46 KNOTS...OR 53 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHWEST WAS MEASURED DURING A THUNDERSTORM BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 149 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVEL.
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...OR 46 MPH...WAS MEASURED DURING A THUNDERSTORM BY AN AUTOMATED STATION AT BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...OR 45 MPH...WAS MEASURED DURING A THUNDERSTORM AT THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE OFFICE IN KEY WEST.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS MEASURED DURING A THUNDERSTORM BY THE AUTOMATED STATION AT KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT C-MAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION KEY WEST ON BOCA CHICA KEY.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ONE MILE SOUTH OF RAMROD KEY MOVING EAST. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT A CWOP AUTOMATED STATION AT THE FLORIDA KEYS AQUEDUCT AUTHORITY PUMP STATION ON RAMROD KEY.
A WIND GUST TO 44 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT A CWOP AUTOMATED STATION ON CUDJOE KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.

A TORNADO WAS REPORTED BY MONROE COUNTY SHERIFFS DISPATCH. A PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION WAS CONDUCTED BY A FORMER EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT DIRECTOR AND FORMER CITY MANAGER. NUMEROUS REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO TREES...PALM FRONDS AND BRANCHES IN WIRES. THREE HOMES WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. TORNADO WAS A WATERSPOUT THAT MOVED SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST FROM EASTERN BOOT KEY TO MARATHON HIGH SCHOOL. ESTIMATED EF0 DAMAGE RATING.
A WIND GUST OF 46 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE VOICE OF AMERICA RADIO STATION BETWEEN BOOT KEY AND FLAMINGO ISLAND IN MARATHON.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT A CWOP AUTOMATED STATION AT THE FLORIDA KEYS AQUEDUCT AUTHORITY PUMP STATION ON LONG KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE MARATHON AIRPORT ASOS.
A SUSTAINED WIND OF 37 KNOTS WITH GUSTS TO 49 KNOTS WAS OBSERVED AT THE FIRE STATION ON UPPER MATECUMBE KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT A WEATHERFLOW AUTOMATED STATION ON UPPER MATECUMBE KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE CWOP AUTOMATED STATION AT THE MONTESSORI SCHOOL ON PLANTATION KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 48 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
A STORM CHASER OBSERVED A WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF GRASSY KEY.
THE PUBLIC OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT JUST SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY.
AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF UPPER SUGARLOAF KEY. DURATION 22 MINUTES.
CMAN REGISTERED A 37 KNOT WIND GUST

NAVY WEATHER OBSERVER AND A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT 3 MILES SOUTHEAST OF BOCA CHICA NAS. NAVY OBSERVER NOTED WATERSPOUT DEMISE AT 1053 AM AS WATERSPOUT APPROACHED RAINSHAFT. DURATION 6 MINUTES.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS RECORDED AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM.
A WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH OF 41 KNOTS...OR 47 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL C-MAN STATION DURING THE PASSAGE OF A SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH OF 38 KNOTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION...DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION...DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH OF 47 KNOTS...OR 54 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION...DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION...DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
MONROE COUNTY FIRE AND RESCUE PERSONNEL REPORTED TWO WATERSPOUTS IN A CLOUD LINE NORTH OF MARATHON. MOVEMENT AND DURATION UNKNOWN.
WIND GUST MEASURED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT OF 44 KNOTS FROM A PULSE THUNDERSTORM.
AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF SMATHERS BEACH IN KEY WEST.
WIND GUST MEASURED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT OF 47 KNOTS FROM A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
WIND GUST MEASURED AT SAND KEY LIGHT OF 35 KNOTS FROM A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT WITH AN ASSOCIATED SPRAY RING WHILE DRIVING SOUTHWEST ON THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE BETWEEN PIDGEON KEY AND OHIO KEY.
A NAVY WEATHER OBSERVER AT BOCA CHICA OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF THE BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE AND A NATIONAL MARINE SANCTUARY SPOTTER ABOUT 8 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. MOVEMENT WAS A VERY SLOW DRIFT TOWARD THE WEST. DURATION 8 MINUTES.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE AND A NATIONAL MARINE SANCTUARY SPOTTER ABOUT 8 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. MOVEMENT WAS A VERY SLOW DRIFT TOWARD THE WEST. DURATION 8 MINUTES.

AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE OBSERVED TWO WATERSPOUTS ABOUT 6 MILES NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. NO SPRAY RINGS WERE VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS UNKNOWN. DURATION OF ONE WATERSPOUT WAS 9 MINUTES. DURATION OF THE OTHER WATERSPOUT WAS 19 MINUTES.
WATER SPOUT LASTED 16 MINUTES
AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 8 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED ABOUT 6 MILES NORTHWEST OF BOCA CHICA BY THE OBSERVER AT BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE NORTHWEST. DURATION 7 MINUTES.
A COMMERCIAL FISHERMAN SPOTTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF LOOE KEY LIGHT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS WELL-DEFINED WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION UNKNONW.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT SAND KEY LIGHT AT 252 PM EDT.
A BOATER REPORTED SEVERAL WATER SPOUTS IN A LINE EXTENDING NORTHEAST FROM THE CONTENT KEYS. ALL SPOUTS LASTEST LESS THAN 20 MINUTES.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST TO 39 KNOTS
A SMALL WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED OVER FLORIDA BAY NORTH OF CONCH KEY. THE SPOUT DISSIPATED AT 939 AM EDT. DURATION UNKNOWN. MOVEMENT UNKNOWN.
CMAN STATION SANF1 MEASURED THUNDERSTORMS WIND GUSTS OF 39 KNOTS.

A WATERSPOUT REPORTED BY FAA-CONTRACT TOWER/OBSERVER PERSONNEL AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. ADDITIONALLY, THE OBSERVER AT NAS BOCA CHICA REPORTED THE SAME WATERSPOUT. THE WATERSPOUT LASTED FOR ABOUT 17 MINUTES.
AIRPORT TOWER PERSONNEL OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT WITH A DURATION OF 4 MINUTES.
PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM REPORTED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS.

A WEATHER OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL STATION ON BOCA CHICA AND A SECOND OBSERVER AT KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 7 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF BOCA CHICA MOVING WEST. ADDITIONALLY, A NAVY PILOT ALSO OBSERVED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS IN THE VICINITY AT THIS TIME. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 18 MINUTES.
AN EAST-SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT C-MAN STATION FROM THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW.
PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT VIA RADIO TO LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT. UNKNOWN DURATION OF WATERSPOUT.
NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED WATERSPOUT WHILE INFLATING UPPER AIR BALLOON. WATER SPOUT OVER EASTERN DRY ROCKS MOVING SW AT 4 KTS.
A NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED AN APPARENT WATERSPOUT 7 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY AND DISSIPATED AT 236 PM EDT.
A NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED AN APPARENT WATERSPOUT 7 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY AND DISSIPATED AT 246 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION FROM A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW.
SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT HAD JUST DISSIPATED. THE SPOUT LASTED LESS THAN 10 MINUTES.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A LARGE WATERSPOUT JUST NORTHWEST OF LONG KEY MOVING NORTHWEST. A FUNNEL CLOUD FORMED AROUND 538 PM EDT, OVER THE CHANNEL 5 BRIDGE AND THEN MOVED OFF TO THE NORTHWEST WHERE IT FURTHER DEVELOPED INTO A WATERSPOUT. DURATION WAS AT LEAST 10 MINUTES, BUT THE TIME IT DISSIPATED IS UNKNOWN.
AN EASTERLY WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION FROM THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW.
A WEST NORTHWESTERLY WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION FROM A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW.
A WATERSPOUT EXTENDING TO THE SEA SRUFACE WAS REPORTED 2 MILES NORTH OF MILE MARKER 79 BY LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION AT 322 PM EDT.
TWO OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEES OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT 2 MILES OFFSHORE OF BIG COPPITT KEY. AT 450 PM THE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED TO BE DISSIPATING.
A DISSIPATING WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC 5 MILES NORTH NORTHWEST OF NEWFOUND HARBOR CHANNEL.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WELL DEFINED WATERSPOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE. THE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED TO HAVE DISSIPATED AT 504 PM EDT.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT MOVED ONSHORE AND RAPIDLY DISSIPATED ON THE BAYSIDE OF LONG KEY NEAR MILE MARKER 69. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT MOVED ONSHORE AND RAPIDLY DISSIPATED ON THE BAYSIDE OF LONG KEY NEAR MILE MARKER 69. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A LARGE FUNNEL CLOUD OVER BOW CHANNEL...NEAR THE KOA CAMPGROUND ON SUGARLOAF KEY. THE FUNNEL CLOUD BECAME OBSCURED BY RAIN CLOUDS AROUND 312 PM EDT.

A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A WELL-DEVELOPED SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED ABOUT 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF BAHIA HONDA STATE PARK. THE WATERSPOUT MOVED LITTLE AT FIRST...THEN ACCELERATED WESTWARD UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY. DURATION 17 MINUTES.

A SHORT-LIVED FUNNEL CLOUD WAS OBSERVED BY A STORM SPOTTER JUST NORTH OF UPPER SUGARLOAF KEY. DURATION 1 MINUTE. THE FUNNEL DEVELOPED BETWEEN TWO SHOWERS AND APPARENTLY WEAKENED RAPIDLY AS THE EASTERN SHOWER INTENSIFIED.
A SHORT-LIVED FUNNEL CLOUD WAS OBSERVED BY AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST ADJACENT TO A DEVELOPING THUNDERSTORM WEST-NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. DURATION 1 MINUTE.
A SHORT-LIVED FUNNEL CLOUD WAS OBSERVED BY AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST ADJACENT TO A DEVELOPING THUNDERSTORM WEST OF KEY WEST. DURATION 1 MINUTE.
AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A BRIEF FUNNEL CLOUD, WHICH EXTENDED LESS THAN A QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN TO THE HORIZON. THE DURATION OF THE FUNNEL CLOUD WAS AROUND 3 MINUTES.

AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A BRIEF FUNNEL CLOUD, WHICH EXTENDED ABOUT A QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE HORIZON. THE FUNNEL CLOUD HAD A DURATION OF LESS THAN A MINUTE BEFORE DISSIPATING.
CONTRACT WEATHER OBSERVER AT NAVAL AIR STATION BOCA CHICA RELAYED A REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT FROM THE TOWER PERSONNEL AT NAS BOCA CHICA. DURATION 32 MINUTES.
STORM CHASER REPORTED A BRIEF WATERSPOUT WHICH DID SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO A SMALL BOAT. WATERSPOUT DURATION REPORTED AS 2 MINUTES.
OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION BOCA CHICA WEATHER OFFICE RELAYED A REPORT FROM THEIR TOWER PERSONNEL OF A WATERSPOUT.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF THE 7 MILE BRIDGE EMBEDDED IN A CLUSTER OF SHOWERS. SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.
A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A BRIEF WATERSPOUT ABOUT 2 MILES EAST OF AMERICAN SHOAL LIGHT.
FUNNEL CLOUD REPORTED HALFWAY DOWN TO THE WATER FOR THE PAST 10 MINUTES. NO SPRAY RING VISIBLE.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT A CWOP AUTOMATED STATION AT THE FLORIDA KEYS AQUEDUCT AUTHORITY PUMP STATION ON LONG KEY.

A WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS...OR 51 MPH...FROM THE NORTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION AS A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 52 FEET ABOVE MEAN SEA LEVEL.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ON THE GULF SIDE OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE. THE EXACT TIME OF THE EVENT WAS NOT AVAILABLE. LISTED IS THE TIME OF THE ACTUAL PHONE CALL TO THE NWS OFFICE. DURATION AND MOVEMENT WERE ALSO NOT AVAILABLE.
THE MONROE COUNTY SHERIFFS DEPARTMENT REPORTED A POSSIBLE TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR 4TH STREET. THERE WAS A TREE DOWN BLOCKING THIS ROAD. THE TIME OF THE EVENT WAS ESTIMATED AT 1 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS FROM THE WEST-NORTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION DURING A PASSING THUNDERSTORM. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 52 FEET ABOVE MEAN SEA LEVEL.
A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED A FUNNEL CLOUD OVER HAWK CHANNEL SOUTH OF RAMROD KEY. DURATION UNKNOWN.
KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT TOWER PERSONNEL REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 6 MILES SOUTHEAST OF THE TOWER...BEGINNING AT 609 PM AND ENDING AT 610 PM.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS REPORTED AT 919 PM BY A C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION AT 542 AM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED DURING A PASSING THUNDERSTORM AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 159 FEET ABOVE MSL.

A WIND GUST OF 48 KTS...OR 55 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT...AT 1048 EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST SOUTHEAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAINBAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.

A WIND GUST OF 44 KTS...OR 51 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 1112 AM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE SOUTHEAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAINBAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.

A WIND GUSTS OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 1238 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST SOUTHEAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAINBAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.

A WIND GUST OF 36 KTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 303 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST SOUTHEAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAIN BAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.
A WIND GUST OF 46 KTS...OR 53 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 312 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAIN BAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.

A WIND GUST OF 38 KTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 336 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAIN BAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON LONG KEY LIGHT AT 351 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING RAIN BAND FROM HURRICANE GUSTAV.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 926 PM EDT.

A TORNADO WAS REPORTED BY THE MONROE COUNTY SHERIFFS DEPARTMENT NEAR GRASSY ROAD IN KEY LARGO...MILE MARKER 106. THE INTENSITY OF THE TORNADO IS UNKNOWN BUT BELIEVED TO BE BETWEEN F0 AND F1 INTENSITY. ASSESSMENT TO FOLLOW.

POSSIBLE TORNADO REPORTED BY ISLAMORADA FIRE AND RESCUE PERSONNEL IN SAFETY HARBOR SUBDIVISION OF LOWER MATECUMBE KEY. POWER LINES AND TREE BRANCHES REPORTED DOWNED...ALONG WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO ONE HOME.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY TRAINED SPOTTERS JUST SOUTH OF CUDJOE KEY. DURATION 15 MINUTES. THE WATERSPOUT WAS LOCATED IMMEDIATELY ADJACENT TO A RAIN SHAFT.
AIR TRAFFIC CONTROLLERS AT KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT OBSERVED A BRIEF FUNNEL CLOUD ABOUT 3 MILES EAST OF THE AIRPORT...OR IN THE VICINITY OF BOCA CHICA CHANNEL. DURATION 2 MINUTES.
A MATURE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER SOUTH OF SUMMERLAND KEY. DURATION AND MOVEMENT UNKNOWN.

A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT OVER LOWER SUGARLOAF CHANNEL SOUTH OF MILE MARKER 17. DURATION 2 MINUTES. THE WATERSPOUT APPARENTLY MOVED WESTWARD AND MADE LANDFALL AS A WEAK TORNADO AT BAY POINT BEFORE QUICKLY DISSIPATING.
A WATERSPOUT MOVED WEST FROM LOWER SUGARLOAF CHANNEL TO BAY POINT...QUICKLY DISSIPATING AFTER TURNING OVER POTTED PLANTS AND TRASH CANS NEAR ONE HOME ON BAY DRIVE.
A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING OVER HAWK CHANNEL...MOVING SLOWLY WESTWARD. DURATION 10 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 450 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS FROM THE EAST AND WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
39 KNOT WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM REPORTED AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT.
AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED THREE SHORT LIVED WATERSPOUTS NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. IT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A CLOUD LINE.

AT 1135 AM EDT AN NWS EMPLOYEE AT THE WEATHER FORECAST OFFICE IN KEY WEST OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF BIG COPPITT KEY MOVING WEST AT ABOUT 8 KNOTS. THE WATERSPOUT WAS VERY NARROW AND ROPE-LIKE AND THEN DISSIPATED AT 1139 AM. AT 1126 AM A CERTIFIED OBSERVER AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT OBSERVED A FUNNEL CLOUD ABOUT 5 MILES TO THE EAST. THE FUNNEL CLOUD DISSIPATED AT 1129 AM.
A LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT ON THE OCEANSIDE OF MILE MARKER 85. DISTANCE OFFSHORE WAS UNKNOWN. DISSIPATION OF THE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED AT 336 PM EDT.

A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING AT THE SEA SURFACE ABOUT 6 MILES SOUTHEAST OF LOWER MATECUMBE KEY OFFSHORE MILE MARKER 78. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED BENEATH A CLOUD LINE...AND EVENTUALLY DISSIPATED AS A RAIN SHAFT OVERTOOK THE FUNNEL. MOVEMENT WAS EASTWARD 5 TO 10 KNOTS. DURATION 23 MINUTES.
SPOTTER CALLED IN WITH REPORT OF WATERSPOUT NEAR MILE MARKER 73 ON BAYSIDE.SPOTTER CALLED BACK AT 110 PM AND REPORTED SPOUT HAD DISSIPATED. DURATION OF EVENT WAS 10 MINUTES.

THE NWS FORECAST OFFICE IN KEY WEST OBSERVED A FUNNEL CLOUD DEVELOP JUST OFF THE SOUTHEAST SHORE OF SIGSBEE PARK THIS AFTERNOON...AROUND 352 PM EDT. A SPRAY RING EMERGED FROM BEHIND A VISUAL OBSTRUCTION AS IT DEVELOPED INTO A MATURE WATERSPOUT. THE WATERSPOUT MOVED WEST-SOUTHWESTWARD OVER GARRISON BIGHT CHANNEL...BETWEEN SIGSBEE PARK AND FLEMING KEY. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED WHEN IT BRIEFLY CAME ASHORE ON FLEMING KEY...AND THEN DISSIPATED OVER MAN OF WAR HARBOR. DURATION 19 MINUTES.

A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER OVER HAWK CHANNEL 2 MILES SOUTH OF INDIAN KEY. THE WATERSPOUT MOVED NORTH AND DISSIPATED OVER INDIAN KEY AT 421 PM EDT. TOTAL DURATION 14 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL C-MAN STATION DURING A THUNDERSTORM.

TWO REPORTS FROM THE PUBLIC INDICATED A SLIM MATURE WATERSPOUT FORMED ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY THIS EVENING...NEAR LOOE KEY LIGHT. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE FOR A SHORT PERIOD OF TIME. DUE TO DARKNESS AND THE PRESENCE OF HEAVY SHOWERS VISUAL CONFIRMATION OF THE SPOUT WAS LOST AROUND 727 PM EDT AND IT WAS SUSPECTED TO HAVE DISSIPATED SHORTLY THEREAFTER. DURATION ABOUT 10 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE EAST SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...OR 46 MPH...FROM THE EAST SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT MOLASSES REEF C-MAN PLATFORM DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...OR 45 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN PLATFORM DURING A THUNDERSTORM.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS INTIALLY OBSERVED AT 242 PM AND REPORTED AT 247 PM. THE WATERSPOUT WAS DESCRIBED AS VERY NARROW, NEARLY STATIONARY AND EXTENDED NEARLY ALL THE WAY TO THE HORIZON.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 10 MILES SOUTH SOUTHEAST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT EXTENDED ABOUT HALFWAY DOWN TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED DUE TO AN OBSTRUCTED VIEW. THE INITIAL CONDENSATION FUNNEL RECEDED TO THE CLOUD BASE AT 424 PM THEN REFORMED A THIN FUNNEL EXTENDING DOWN TO THE HORIZON. MOVEMENT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY AND DISSIPATED AT 428 PM.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED WITHIN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE AND EXTENDED HALFWAY TO THE HORIZON. TREES OBSTRUCTED THE VIEW OF THE OCEAN SURFACE MASKING ANY VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THE WATERSPOUT DRIFTED WESTWARD AND DISSIPATED AT 458 PM. DURATION 8 MINUTES.

A WEST-SOUTHWESTERLY WIND GUST OF 43 KTS...OR 49 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 556 PM EDT. WINDS AT THE 600 PM OBSERVATION WERE FROM THE WEST AT 37 KTS...OR 43 MPH. THESE WINDS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH A PRE-FRONTAL TROUGH.

A WEST-SOUTHWESTERLY WIND GUST OF 44 KTS...OR 51 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 607 PM EDT. WINDS AT THE 700 PM OBSERVATION WERE FROM THE SOUTHWEST AT 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH. THESE WINDS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH A PRE-FRONTAL TROUGH.
